---
title: "Building from Source"
---

# Building from Source

This page covers the prerequisites and commands for building Mayon from source.

## Prerequisites

| Tool | Version | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Node.js | 22 | Set via `.nvmrc`. Use `nvm install && nvm use`. |
| pnpm | 10 | Set in `packageManager`. Enable with `corepack enable && corepack prepare pnpm@10 --activate`. |
| Docker | — | Required to run Postgres and the Mayon server. |

No bun.

## Commands

| Command | What it does |
|---|---|
| `pnpm install` | Install Node.js dependencies. |
| `pnpm dev` | Run the SvelteKit SPA dev server at http://localhost:5173 (requires `pnpm dev:deps`). |
| `pnpm dev:deps` | Start Postgres + server in Docker (deps for `pnpm dev`). |
| `docker compose up` | Run the full stack: web SPA + server + Postgres (web on :8080). |
| `pnpm build` | Build the SPA into `build/`. |
| `pnpm check` | Type-check with `svelte-check`. |
| `pnpm lint` | ESLint (flat config) + Prettier `--check`. |
| `pnpm format` | Prettier `--write`. |
| `pnpm test` | Vitest against the pglite (Postgres) test driver — runs once. |
| `pnpm test:watch` | Vitest in watch mode. |
| `pnpm --filter @mayon/server test` | Vitest for the server package. |
| `pnpm db:generate` | Generate a new drizzle migration from `src/lib/db/schema.ts` into `drizzle/`. |
| `pnpm db:studio` | Open Drizzle Studio against the schema. |

## Development workflow

The typical inner-loop for frontend work:

```bash
pnpm dev:deps   # start Postgres + server in Docker
pnpm dev        # SvelteKit SPA at http://localhost:5173
```

This starts the SvelteKit dev server with HMR. Open http://localhost:5173. Changes to `.svelte`, `.ts`, and CSS files are reflected instantly. The dev server proxies `/api` requests to the Dockerized server.

For the full stack:

```bash
docker compose up   # web on :8080, server + Postgres in containers
```

## Testing

Tests use a pglite (Postgres-compatible) test driver — no Docker or browser needed. Run tests with:

```bash
pnpm test
```

Tests cover the repository layer, provider adapters, context assembly, transport contracts, and key storage.
